terms.definition: AI systems whose main capability is creating new data—text, images, audio, code, or other modalities—by sampling from learned generative models.
Short industry label for Generative AI; often refers to consumer and developer tools that synthesize content (chat, image, code generation).
A model that represents a data distribution (or conditional distribution) and can produce new samples; contrast with models trained mainly to classify or regress.
A model that estimates P(label | input) or a decision boundary; it predicts attributes rather than synthesizing full new examples.
A large model pretrained on broad data and adapted to many downstream tasks; many foundation models are generative at inference, though not all deployments use generation.
